name: Balaguer - Sant Llorenç - Camarasa - Ermita Sant Jordi - El Merengue — Route through the Noguera
name_original: Balaguer - Sant Llorenç - Camarasa - Ermita Sant Jordi - El Merengue — Ruta por la Noguera
region_intro: Mountain biking around Balaguer offers a diverse landscape characterized by the Noguera river valley, rolling hills, and the Sierra de Bellmunt mountain range. The region features a mix of open terrain, forested sections, and routes that pass by natural water features like lakes. Elevation changes are common, providing varied challenges for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Balaguer The most popular mountain bike trail is Balaguer - Sant Llorenç - Camarasa - Ermita Sant Jordi - El Merengue — Route through the Noguera, a 32.4 miles (52.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 28 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Noguera region with significant elevation gains. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Balaguer - Sierra de Bellmunt - Penelles — Ruta circular, a moderate 22.1 miles (35.5 km) path. This circular route offers views across the Sierra de Bellmunt. Local mountain bikers also love the Monasterio de les Avellanes – Village Lake loop from Balaguer, a 41.5 miles (66.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain including a monastery and a village lake, often completed in about 6 hours 35 minutes. Mountain biking around Balaguer is defined by river valleys, rolling hills, and natural lakes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult climbs. The routes in Balaguer are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Balaguer's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Balaguer?
A: The mountain bike trails around Balaguer offer a diverse landscape. You'll find routes traversing the Noguera river valley, rolling hills, and sections within the Sierra de Bellmunt mountain range. The terrain includes a mix of open areas, forested sections, and paths that pass by natural water features like lakes. Expect varied elevation changes, providing different levels of challenge.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Balaguer?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails around Balaguer listed on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, with options ranging from moderate paths to more difficult climbs.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails for beginners in Balaguer?
A: Yes, Balaguer offers several easier mountain bike trails suitable for beginners. Out of the over 50 routes, 8 are classified as easy, providing a good starting point for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Balaguer?
A: Yes, Balaguer has several circular mountain bike routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e946230292/balaguer-sierra-de-bellmunt-penelles-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balaguer - Sierra de Bellmunt - Penelles — Ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option offering views across the Sierra de Bellmunt. Another challenging circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10467515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de les Avellanes – Village Lake loop from Balaguer&lt;/a&gt;, which includes varied terrain and takes you past a monastery and a village lake.

Q: What are some natural attractions or landmarks I can see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many mountain bike routes in Balaguer pass by interesting natural features and landmarks. You might encounter sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4131150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova del Tabac&lt;/a&gt; cave, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7079428" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mas de Melons Wildlife Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1000424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Lorenzo de Montgai Dam&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails also lead to viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1045778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pala Alta Summit&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1035484" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bull Rock&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or religious sites accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, some mountain bike routes in Balaguer offer access to historical and religious sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10467515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de les Avellanes – Village Lake loop from Balaguer&lt;/a&gt; takes you past a monastery. You can also find routes that pass by hermitages like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1042597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Jordi Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1039653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mare de Déu de Montalegre Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, and even the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4966758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman settlement at Cima del Monteró&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Balaguer?
A: The mountain bike trails in Balaguer are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Over 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, from river valleys to rolling hills and natural lakes.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Balaguer?
A: Balaguer's climate generally allows for mountain biking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides are often preferred. Winters are usually mild, making it possible to ride, though some higher elevation trails might be cooler.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Balaguer offers 16 routes classified as difficult, providing significant challenges for experienced mountain bikers. A notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943535952/balaguer-sant-llorenc-camarasa-ermita-sant-jordi-el-merengue-route-through-the-noguera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balaguer - Sant Llorenç - Camarasa - Ermita Sant Jordi - El Merengue — Route through the Noguera&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 50 km with substantial elevation gains.

Q: Is there a route that combines gravel sections with other terrain?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6954701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel climb near Balaguer – Balaguer Bridge loop from Balaguer&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route that features gravel sections, offering a varied riding experience for those who enjoy mixed terrain.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by lakes or other water features?
A: Yes, the region's landscape includes natural water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10467515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de les Avellanes – Village Lake loop from Balaguer&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40327906" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Village Lake – Sant Jordi Hermitage loop from Balaguer&lt;/a&gt; are examples of routes that pass by a village lake, offering scenic views and a refreshing element to your ride.
tips: Also, Sant Miquel bridge. It crosses the Segre river as it passes through Balaguer. Links the old town and the new town
Located at the top of the Fues mountain, we have a good panoramic view of the Segre river valley and the pre-Pyrenean mountain ranges. At our feet, we have the beautiful and quiet town of Gerb.

Good views of Camarasa and the Segre river. The hermitage of Sant Jordi was built in the 19th century to replace a previous temple in ruins that can be found half a km from the current one to the north. The interior is simple with an image of Sant Jordi
From this road you have beautiful views of the Sant Llorenç de Montgai reservoir.
Place of historical interest and free access. Civil War defensive position with restored trenches, tunnels and ammunition storage sites. Very good views of the Noguera.

waypoints.name: View of Gerb and the Segre Valley from Mirador de Fues
View of the Sant Llorenç de Montgai reservoir
